strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, comfortably exceeding the 15-year minimum
Demonstrated entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective, shipped a top-10 Apple TV app at WatchUp, and is currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Active knowledge-sharing through conference talks, multi-day workshops, and open-source projects, directly aligning with the role's requirement to inspire others internally and externally
concerns: AI expertise is nascent and narrowly scoped — MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling at a single company since April 2025 falls well short of 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI'

Career trajectory plateaus at Senior/Staff Software Developer throughout; no evidence of director-level scope, strategic customer advisory, or cross-organizational influence commensurate with a director-level IC role

No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no cloud platform strategy experience at scale — both preferred qualifications that function as near-requirements for a Principal Engineer in AI Strategy at this seniority
response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine breadth — two decades of polyglot engineering, open-source leadership, and recent hands-on work with agentic tooling — but the role demands a director-level strategist with recognized, deep AI expertise and a proven track record of advising enterprise customers on cloud and AI adoption at scale. His AI exposure is limited to roughly one year of infrastructure tooling work, his title progression has stayed in the senior/staff band, and there is no evidence of the large-scale customer-facing influence or AI thought leadership (publications, keynotes, AI products shipped at scale) that this position requires.
